comment_text.sn7.2:2.1: Apparently a reference to the previous Sutta.
comment_text.sn7.2:7.1: Read ubhinnaṁ tikicchantaṁ taṁ per Thag 6.12:4.1.
comment_text.sn7.2:9.4: He is no longer called “the Rude”.

root_text.sn7.2:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn7.2:2.1: “bhāradvājagotto kira brāhmaṇo samaṇassa gotamassa santike agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajito”ti kupito anattamano yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ asabbhāhi pharusāhi vācāhi akkosati paribhāsati. Evaṁ vutte, bhagavā akkosakabhāradvājaṁ brāhmaṇaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn7.2:2.3: api nu kho te āgacchanti mittāmaccā ñātisālohitā atithiyo”ti?
root_text.sn7.2:2.4: “Appekadā me, bho gotama, āgacchanti mittāmaccā ñātisālohitā atithiyo”ti.
root_text.sn7.2:2.5: “Taṁ kiṁ maññasi, brāhmaṇa, api nu tesaṁ anuppadesi khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā sāyanīyaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn7.2:2.6: “Appekadā nesāhaṁ, bho gotama, anuppademi khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā sāyanīyaṁ vā”ti.
root_text.sn7.2:2.7: “Sace kho pana te, brāhmaṇa, nappaṭiggaṇhanti, kassa taṁ hotī”ti?
root_text.sn7.2:2.8: “Sace te, bho gotama, nappaṭiggaṇhanti, amhākameva taṁ hotī”ti.
root_text.sn7.2:2.9: “Evameva kho, brāhmaṇa, yaṁ tvaṁ amhe anakkosante akkosasi, arosente rosesi, abhaṇḍante bhaṇḍasi, taṁ te mayaṁ nappaṭiggaṇhāma.
root_text.sn7.2:3.1: Yo kho, brāhmaṇa, akkosantaṁ paccakkosati, rosentaṁ paṭiroseti, bhaṇḍantaṁ paṭibhaṇḍati, ayaṁ vuccati, brāhmaṇa, sambhuñjati vītiharatīti.
root_text.sn7.2:3.2: Te mayaṁ tayā neva sambhuñjāma na vītiharāma.
root_text.sn7.2:3.5: “Bhavantaṁ kho gotamaṁ sarājikā parisā evaṁ jānāti:
root_text.sn7.2:3.7: Atha ca pana bhavaṁ gotamo kujjhatī”ti.
root_text.sn7.2:8.1: Evaṁ vutte, akkosakabhāradvājo brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn7.2:8.3: esāhaṁ bhavantaṁ gotamaṁ saraṇaṁ gacchāmi dhammañca bhikkhusaṅghañca.
root_text.sn7.2:8.4: Labheyyāhaṁ, bhante, bhoto gotamassa santike pabbajjaṁ, labheyyaṁ upasampadan”ti.
root_text.sn7.2:9.1: Alattha kho akkosakabhāradvājo brāhmaṇo bhagavato santike pabbajjaṁ, alattha upasampadaṁ.
root_text.sn7.2:9.2: Acirūpasampanno kho panāyasmā akkosakabhāradvājo eko vūpakaṭṭho appamatto ātāpī pahitatto viharanto nacirasseva—yassatthāya kulaputtā sammadeva agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajanti tadanuttaraṁ—brahmacariyapariyosānaṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja vihāsi.
root_text.sn7.2:9.3: “Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ nāparaṁ itthattāyā”ti abbhaññāsi.
root_text.sn7.2:9.4: Aññataro ca panāyasmā bhāradvājo arahataṁ ahosīti.

translation_text.sn7.2:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn7.2:1.2: The brahmin Bhāradvāja the Rude heard a rumor that
translation_text.sn7.2:2.1: a brahmin of the Bhāradvāja clan had gone forth from the lay life to homelessness in the presence of the ascetic Gotama. Angry and displeased he went to the Buddha and abused and insulted him with rude, harsh words. When he had spoken, the Buddha said to him:
translation_text.sn7.2:2.3: Do friends and colleagues, relatives and kin, and guests still come to visit you?”
translation_text.sn7.2:2.5: “Do you then serve them with fresh and cooked foods and savories?”
translation_text.sn7.2:2.7: “But if they don’t accept it, brahmin, who does it belong to?”
translation_text.sn7.2:2.8: “In that case it still belongs to me.”
translation_text.sn7.2:2.9: “In the same way, brahmin, when you abuse, harass, and attack us who do not abuse, harass, and attack, we don’t accept it.
translation_text.sn7.2:2.10: It still belongs to you, brahmin,
translation_text.sn7.2:3.1: Someone who, when abused, harassed, and attacked, abuses, harasses, and attacks in return is said to eat the food and have a reaction to it.
translation_text.sn7.2:3.2: But we neither eat your food nor do we have a reaction to it.
translation_text.sn7.2:3.3: It still belongs to you, brahmin,
translation_text.sn7.2:3.5: “The king and his retinue believe of the worthy Gotama,
translation_text.sn7.2:3.6: ‘the ascetic Gotama is a perfected one’.
translation_text.sn7.2:3.7: And yet he still gets angry.”
translation_text.sn7.2:4.1: “Whence anger for one free of anger,
translation_text.sn7.2:5.1: When you get angry at an angry person
translation_text.sn7.2:5.2: you just make things worse for yourself.
translation_text.sn7.2:5.3: When you don’t get angry at an angry person
translation_text.sn7.2:5.4: you win a battle hard to win.
translation_text.sn7.2:6.1: When you know that the other is angry,
translation_text.sn7.2:6.2: you act for the good of both
translation_text.sn7.2:6.4: if you’re mindful and stay calm.
translation_text.sn7.2:8.1: When he had spoken, Bhāradvāja the Rude said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn7.2:8.3: I go for refuge to the worthy Gotama, to the teaching, and to the mendicant Saṅgha.
translation_text.sn7.2:8.4: Sir, may I receive the going forth, the ordination in the worthy Gotama’s presence?”
translation_text.sn7.2:9.1: And the brahmin Bhāradvāja the Rude received the going forth, the ordination in the Buddha’s presence.
translation_text.sn7.2:9.2: Not long after his ordination, Venerable Bhāradvāja the Rude, living alone, withdrawn, diligent, keen, and resolute, soon realized the supreme end of the spiritual path in this very life. He lived having achieved with his own insight the goal for which gentlemen rightly go forth from the lay life to homelessness.
translation_text.sn7.2:9.3: He understood: “Rebirth is ended; the spiritual journey has been completed; what had to be done has been done; there is nothing further for this place.”
translation_text.sn7.2:9.4: And Venerable Bhāradvāja became one of the perfected.
